page: Amazon Web Services m4.2xlarge is a general-purpose, fourth-generation virtual server instance running on the Xen hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor. The instance provides 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 2.4 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and features a 45 MB L3 cache. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPU hardware. Network connectivity is limited to a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the m4.2xlarge exhibits mixed performance characteristics. Single-core CPU performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% across stress-ng, Geekbench, and compression tests. However, multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks, including both small-block cached and large-block uncached read and write operations, rank in the average tier. Passmark CPU and memory scores also align with this average tier, though database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput remain in the bottom 25%. For machine learning workloads, LLM text generation using a small 135M model achieves average speeds, but performance drops to poor when executing text generation on a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m4.2xlarge represents an older hardware class that offers a balanced resource density of vCPU to memory, but lacks modern instruction sets like AVX-512. The primary tradeoffs include the absence of local NVMe storage, a low baseline network speed of 1 Gbps, and weak single-threaded execution speeds. This instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads that benefit from multi-threaded processing and moderate memory capacity without requiring high single-core throughput or local disk I/O, such as basic web serving, development environments, and moderate multi-threaded background processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m4.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server built on the Xen hypervisor and powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show weak single-core CPU execution and database operations, placing them in the bottom 25%, while multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth read and write speeds remain in the average tier. Qualitatively, this older-generation instance offers a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio but presents tradeoffs in single-threaded speed and network throughput. It is suited for general-purpose workloads, moderate multi-threaded processing, and development environments that do not require high-speed local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m4.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 8 vCPUs, 32.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor, delivering average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m4.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server with 8 vCPUs, 32.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor.
tagline: An x86_64 general-purpose virtual server offering balanced memory and dedicated vCPUs for moderate multi-threaded workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB RAM
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Xen hypervisor architecture with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for general-purpose applications and development environments
